sources.label: STCN quote/news page for 002979 (no new filings Jun 6-9)
Sina announcements list for 002979 (latest items pre-date June 6)
Abnormal-volatility notice after May 15-18 surge (context, not new)
Leadshine CES 2026 humanoid component traction (January, already in thesis)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new filings, orders, or guidance since June 6 and today's +0.29% is pure drift, so yesterday's hold stands. The actuation-purity alpha case (frameless motors, joint modules, dexterous hands at $2.6B cap) is unchanged, but the +30% YTD run and mid-May abnormal-volatility flag mean residual alpha is no larger than it was yesterday.
verdicts.bull: Frameless torque motor deliveries grew >20x in 2025 (120k+ units) with 300k/yr capacity, and dexterous hands/joint modules are now batch-supplying dozens of robot OEMs; Q1 2026 revenue +34.6% shows commercialization, not just story.
verdicts.neutral: At +30% YTD and after a May 15-18 limit-up streak that triggered an exchange volatility notice, much of the near-term humanoid optionality is priced; the RMB1.14B private placement adds capacity but also dilution.
verdicts.bear: Humanoid component orders are still small versus the legacy motion-control base; if dexterous-hand/joint-module wins stay at sample-to-pilot scale, the stock derates back toward an industrial-automation multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosure of a named volume supply agreement with a major humanoid OEM (Tesla/Boston Dynamics-class follow-through from CES 2026 talks) or H1 2026 results showing robot-component revenue inflecting.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding in A-share humanoid suppliers unwinds; placement dilution plus any slip in frameless-motor order momentum compresses the rerated multiple.
